steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: The passage describes fossils of "two previously unknown species of prehistoric penguins" studied by paleontologists. The decisive clue is the phrase "prehistoric" and the context of analyzing fossil bones, which implies these species no longer exist.

steps.title: Match the meaning
steps.content:
Choice B — They are no longer living species. directly aligns with the implication of the fossils being from species that are no longer living.

Choice C contradicts the passage's size comparison: one species was more than three times the size of the emperor penguin, not smaller than penguin species that exist today. The passage does not mention habitat behavior.

A, C, and D either contradict or ignore the fossil/prehistoric context.

That matches Choice B — They are no longer living species..
